articles.title: How China is responding to the stalling of talks between the U.S. and Iran
articles.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Zongyuan Zoe Liu, a senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, about how China views the current crisis in the Middle East

articles.title: After Shooting, Trump Demands Approval for His White House Ballroom
articles.summary: An incident involving an armed man at the White House correspondents’ dinner has the president to renewing his push for a project slowed by litigation.

articles.title: A fast-growing Georgia wildfire tops 31 square miles, with evacuations possible
articles.summary: One of two large wildfires in southeastern Georgia continues to grow and now exceeds 31 square miles.

articles.title: A chaotic White House Correspondents' Dinner, as told by NPR reporters in the room
articles.summary: Top government officials were rushed out of the Washington Hilton after gunshots were heard at the dinner. NPR journalists describe hiding under tables and scrambling for information as the night unfo

articles.title: What Our Reporter Saw During the D.C. Shooting
articles.summary: Our reporter was with President Trump at the White House correspondents’ dinner when a gunman breached security. He describes the frantic scenes that unfolded.

articles.title: What we know about Cole Allen, suspected White House Correspondents' dinner shooter
articles.summary: The 31-year-old teacher and engineer from California sent an alleged message to family members saying that he wanted to target administration officials.

articles.title: Family Detained for Months at Texas ICE Facility Released
articles.summary: Two days after their release, the mother and her five children were again detained during an ICE check-in. A judge halted their removal and returned the family home.

articles.title: Iran and U.S. Sink Into Awkward Limbo of ‘No War, No Peace’
articles.summary: Each side is betting it can last longer than the other, analysts say. But there are risks in a stalemate without a deal.

articles.title: Photos: The aftermath of the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting
articles.summary: A suspect has been arrested after firing shots at a security screening area at the Washington Hilton on Saturday night.

articles.title: The Hard Life of an Immigrant Whose Killing Became a Symbol for Trump
articles.summary: President Trump posted surveillance footage of Nilufa Easmin’s brutal killing by another immigrant to advance his agenda. Behind the rhetoric was a more nuanced story.

articles.title: Thom Tillis Is Prepared to Advance Kevin Warsh After U.S. Drops Fed Inquiry
articles.summary: Senator Thom Tillis said he had received assurances from federal prosecutors that eased his concerns, setting the stage for a key committee vote on Kevin Warsh.

articles.title: Details emerge of alleged shooter at White House correspondents' dinner
articles.summary: The alleged gunman has been identified as Cole Allen, according to two sources familiar with the matter.

articles.title: California Man Identified as Cole Tomas Allen in Custody After Shooting at Dinner Attended by Trump
articles.summary: Shots were fired at the hotel hosting the White House correspondents’ dinner. Authorities said the attack was carried out by a lone gunman who was brought down by the Secret Service.

articles.title: World Press Photo Contest winners cast a lens on resilience, pain and bliss
articles.summary: A selection of prize honorees from the 2026 World Press Photo Contest capture the pain of the past year — but also focus on moments of strength, determination and joy.

articles.title: Trump doubts shooter motivated by Iran war as peace talks on hold
articles.summary: A shooting incident at the White House Correspondent's Dinner took focus away from the war in Iran, as Iran's foreign minister planned to return to Islamabad, the site of previous peace talks.

articles.title: King Charles U.S. visit comes at tense moment in transatlantic relationship
articles.summary: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive Monday for a four day U.S. state visit. Some hope the royal touch can heal the transatlantic rift that's emerged under Trump.

articles.title: The Trump Administration Has Changed Almost Every Aspect of Food Stamps
articles.summary: Legislation and regulatory tweaks enacted over the past year have altered who is eligible, what recipients can buy and how much some receive in benefits, among other changes.

articles.title: In a rare interview, a leader of the world's largest right-wing group talks to NPR
articles.summary: The second-in-command of the RSS, a Hindu nationalist organization in India, rarely speaks to the Western press. Here's what he said about his group's controversial history.

articles.title: Mali reeling after coordinated attacks hit multiple cities
articles.summary: Gunfire and explosions rocked Mali's capital and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups exploited worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

articles.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
articles.summary: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace

articles.title: Vessels seized by the U.S. this week were part of a global 'shadow fleet'
articles.summary: NPR's Juana Summers speaks with Elisabeth Braw, senior fellow at the Atlantic Council, about shadow vessels, after the U.S. military's seizure of two Iranian-linked oil tankers.

articles.title: Kushner and U.S. envoy Witkoff will head to Pakistan for new Iran talks
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister arrived in Islamabad, and the White House says Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will go there Saturday to try to "move the ball forward towards a deal."

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
